title: probabilistic list-target split
manager: Writing `a 50% -> [b c]` today copies 50% onto each edge, which double-counts the moment there are sibling probabilistic edges — the outgoing probabilities stop summing to 100. 6.0 reads the origin percentage as the group's weight, split among its members, which is the mathematically correct meaning. It only changes observable behavior in machines that actually have sibling probabilistic edges, but for those it is the difference between a right and a wrong distribution.
cs: the compiler fan-out at `jssm_compiler.ts:149` changes copy -> normalized-split (inner weights per the §28 all-or-nothing rule); histograph/walk tests move to split expectations; vector `a 50% -> [b 20% c 80%]` => 10/40; manifest entry documents copy->split with the sibling-edge example.
